#!/bin/bash

# Define the audit tools to monitor
audit_tools=(
    /usr/bin/auditctl
    /usr/sbin/auditd
    /usr/bin/aureport
    /usr/bin/ausearch
    /usr/bin/autrace
    /usr/bin/audispd
)

# Ensure AIDE is installed
if ! rpm -q aide &> /dev/null; then
    yum install -y aide
fi

# Backup the original AIDE configuration
if [ ! -f /etc/aide/aide.conf.bak ]; then
    cp /etc/aide/aide.conf /etc/aide/aide.conf.bak
fi

# Function to add or update entries in AIDE configuration
update_aide_config() {
    local tool_path="$1"
    local config_line="/^$tool_path$/d; a $tool_path p+i+n+u+g+s+b+m+c+md5+sha256"

    # Update the AIDE configuration file
    sed -i "$config_line" /etc/aide/aide.conf
}

# Register each audit tool with AIDE
for tool in "${audit_tools[@]}"; do
    if [ -f "$tool" ]; then
        update_aide_config "$tool"
    fi
done

# Initialize AIDE database if it doesn't exist
if [ ! -f /var/lib/aide/aide.db.gz ]; then
    aide --init
    mv /var/lib/aide/aide.db.new.gz /var/lib/aide/aide.db.gz
fi

# Update AIDE database to include new configurations
aide --update

# Ensure AIDE service is enabled and started
systemctl enable aide
systemctl start aide

```bash
#!/bin/bash

# Define the audit tools to monitor
audit_tools=(
    /usr/bin/auditctl
    /usr/sbin/auditd
    /usr/bin/aureport
    /usr/bin/ausearch
    /usr/bin/autrace
    /usr/bin/audispd
)

# Ensure AIDE is installed
if ! rpm -q aide &> /dev/null; then
    yum install -y aide
fi

# Backup the original AIDE configuration
if [ ! -f /etc/aide/aide.conf.bak ]; then
    cp /etc/aide/aide.conf /etc/aide/aide.conf.bak
fi

# Function to add or update entries in AIDE configuration
update_aide_config() {
    local tool_path="$1"
    local config_line="/^$tool_path$/d; a $tool_path p+i+n+u+g+s+b+m+c+md5+sha256"

    # Update the AIDE configuration file
    sed -i "$config_line" /etc/aide/aide.conf
}

# Register each audit tool with AIDE
for tool in "${audit_tools[@]}"; do
    if [ -f "$tool" ]; then

    fi
done
```

#!/bin/bash

# Ensure AIDE is installed
if ! command -v aide &> /dev/null; then
    echo "AIDE is not installed. Please install it first."
    exit 1
fi

# Define the AIDE configuration file path
AIDE_CONF="/etc/aide.conf"

# Check if the AIDE configuration file exists
if [[ ! -f "$AIDE_CONF" ]]; then
    echo "AIDE configuration file not found at $AIDE_CONF"
    exit 1
fi

# Function to add ACL verification to a ruleset
add_acl_to_ruleset() {
    local ruleset="$1"
    local acl_line="acl+r"
    if grep -q "^$ruleset" "$AIDE_CONF"; then
        # Check if the ruleset already includes ACL verification
        if ! grep -q "$acl_line" "$AIDE_CONF"; then
            # Append ACL verification to the ruleset
            sed -i "/^$ruleset/s/$/ $acl_line/" "$AIDE_CONF"
        fi
    else
        echo "Ruleset $ruleset not found in $AIDE_CONF"
    fi
}

# Add ACL verification to common rulesets
add_acl_to_ruleset "/etc"
add_acl_to_ruleset "/usr/bin"
add_acl_to_ruleset "/sbin"
add_acl_to_ruleset "/lib"
add_acl_to_ruleset "/lib64"
add_acl_to_ruleset "/var"
add_acl_to_ruleset "/home"
add_acl_to_ruleset "/root"

# Reload AIDE configuration
aide --init
aide --update

echo "AIDE configuration updated to include ACL verification for specified rulesets."

```bash
#!/bin/bash

# Ensure AIDE is installed
if ! command -v aide &> /dev/null; then
    echo "AIDE is not installed. Please install it first."
    exit 1
fi
```

# Backup the original AIDE configuration file
CONFIG_FILE="/etc/aide.conf"
BACKUP_FILE="${CONFIG_FILE}.bak"
if [ ! -f "$BACKUP_FILE" ]; then
    cp "$CONFIG_FILE" "$BACKUP_FILE"
fi

# Function to enable extended attribute checking in a ruleset
enable_extended_attributes() {
    local RULESET="$1"
    if grep -q "^$RULESET\s*=" "$CONFIG_FILE"; then
        sed -i "/^$RULESET\s*=/s/\(=\s*\)/\1+x/" "$CONFIG_FILE"
    fi
}

# Define common rulesets to modify
RULESETS=("ALL" "NORMAL" "DATA" "LOGFILES" "BINARIES" "SCRIPTS" "CONFIG")

# Apply extended attribute checking to each ruleset
for RULESET in "${RULESETS[@]}"; do
    enable_extended_attributes "$RULESET"
done

# Reload AIDE configuration
aide --init
aide --check

echo "Extended attribute checking has been enabled for all defined rulesets in AIDE."

    echo "FIPS mode is already enabled."

    echo "Updated GRUB configuration."

    echo "GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX already includes fips=1."
fi

# Verify FIPS mode is set to persist
if [[ $(grep "fips=1" /etc/default/grub) ]]; then
    echo "FIPS mode will persist across reboots."
else
    echo "Failed to ensure FIPS mode persists across reboots."
    exit 1
fi

echo "System is now in the federally approved cryptographic operating mode."

# Ensure the system-wide cryptographic policy is set to the default-strength profile

# Check if the current policy is already set to default-strength

if [ -z "$current_policy" ]; then
    # Set the system-wide cryptographic policy to default-strength
    update-crypto-policies --set DEFAULT
fi

# Ensure the policy persists across reboots by updating the configuration file
echo "DEFAULT" > /etc/crypto-policies/config

# Verify the policy has been set correctly
updated_policy=$(update-crypto-policies --show | grep "DEFAULT")

if [ -n "$updated_policy" ]; then
    echo "System-wide cryptographic policy has been successfully set to default-strength."
else
    echo "Failed to set system-wide cryptographic policy to default-strength."
    exit 1
fi

PROTOCOLS_TO_DISABLE=("SSL 3.0" "TLS 1.0" "TLS 1.1" "DTLS 0.9" "DTLS 1.0")

is_protocol_disabled() {
    grep -q "^disable_protocols =.*$1" "$GNUTLS_SYS_CONF"

# Iterate over each protocol to disable

    # Check if the protocol is already disabled
    if ! is_protocol_disabled "$PROTOCOL"; then
        # Append the protocol to the disable_protocols line if it doesn't exist
        sed -i "/^disable_protocols =/ s/$/ $PROTOCOL/" "$GNUTLS_SYS_CONF"

done

# Ensure the disable_protocols line exists, adding it if necessary

    echo "disable_protocols =" >> "$GNUTLS_SYS_CONF"

```bash
#!/bin/bash
```

# Ensure the necessary package is installed
if ! rpm -q pam_pkcs11 > /dev/null; then
    sudo yum install -y pam_pkcs11
fi

# Create or update the PAM configuration for GNOME screensaver
PAM_CONFIG="/etc/pam.d/gnome-screensaver"
if ! grep -q "pam_pkcs11.so" "$PAM_CONFIG"; then
    echo "auth required pam_pkcs11.so" | sudo tee -a "$PAM_CONFIG"
fi

# Ensure the GNOME screensaver service is enabled and started
sudo systemctl enable --now gnome-screensaver.service

# Create or update the dconf profile to enforce screen locking on smartcard removal
DCONF_PROFILE="/etc/dconf/profile/user"
if ! grep -q "user-db:user" "$DCONF_PROFILE"; then
    echo "user-db:user" | sudo tee "$DCONF_PROFILE"
fi

# Set the dconf key to lock the screen on smartcard removal
DCONF_KEY="/org/gnome/desktop/screensaver/lock-delay"
if ! dconf read "$DCONF_KEY" | grep -q "uint32 0"; then
    sudo dconf write "$DCONF_KEY" "uint32 0"
fi

# Ensure the dconf settings are locked to prevent user overrides
DCONF_LOCK="/etc/dconf/db/local.d/locks/00-screensaver-lock"
if ! grep -q "/org/gnome/desktop/screensaver/lock-delay" "$DCONF_LOCK"; then
    echo "/org/gnome/desktop/screensaver/lock-delay" | sudo tee "$DCONF_LOCK"
fi

# Update the dconf database
sudo dconf update

# Ensure GNOME Display Manager does not auto-login any user

# Check if GDM is installed
if ! rpm -q gdm; then
    echo "GDM is not installed. Exiting."
    exit 0
fi

# Backup the original configuration file

if [ ! -f /etc/dconf/profile/user ]; then
    echo "user-db:user" | sudo tee /etc/dconf/profile/user
    echo "system-db:local" | sudo tee -a /etc/dconf/profile/user
fi

# Create the local dconf database directory if it doesn't exist
if [ ! -d /etc/dconf/db/local ]; then
    sudo mkdir -p /etc/dconf/db/local
fi

# Create the dconf overrides directory if it doesn't exist
if [ ! -d /etc/dconf/db/local.d ]; then
    sudo mkdir -p /etc/dconf/db/local.d
fi

# Set the session idle delay to 10 minutes and make it read-only
if [ ! -f /etc/dconf/db/local.d/00-session-idle-delay ]; then
    echo "[org/gnome/desktop/session]" | sudo tee /etc/dconf/db/local.d/00-session-idle-delay
    echo "idle-delay=600" | sudo tee -a /etc/dconf/db/local.d/00-session-idle-delay
    echo "lock-delay=0" | sudo tee -a /etc/dconf/db/local.d/00-session-idle-delay

    echo "/org/gnome/desktop/session/lock-delay" | sudo tee -a /etc/dconf/db/local.d/locks/session-idle-delay
fi

# Define the required banner text
required_banner="You are accessing a U.S. Government (USG) Information System (IS) that is provided for USG-authorized use only.
By using this IS (which includes any device attached to this IS), you consent to the following conditions:
- The USG routinely intercepts and monitors communications on this IS for purposes including, but not limited to, penetration testing, COMSEC monitoring, network operations, and defense.
- At any time, and for any legitimate government purpose, the USG may inspect and seize data stored on this IS.

- Unauthorized attempts to break security or access restricted materials are strictly prohibited and are punishable by law.
- For additional information see: http://www.cyber.mil/Compliance/CompliancePrograms/DoD5200204.html"

# Define the path to the banner file
banner_file="/etc/issue.net"

# Check if the current banner matches the required banner

    echo -e "$required_banner" > "$banner_file"
fi

# Ensure the banner file has the correct permissions

if ! grep -q "^Banner /etc/issue.net$" /etc/ssh/sshd_config; then
    echo "Banner /etc/issue.net" >> /etc/ssh/sshd_config
fi

# Restart the SSH service to apply changes
systemctl restart sshd

    local key="$1"
    local value="$2"
    if grep -q "^$key" "$PAM_CONFIG_FILE"; then
        # If the key exists, update its value

```bash
#!/bin/bash
```

# Ensure the /etc/login.defs file is configured with a minimum of 5000 hashing rounds for SHA passwords
if ! grep -q '^ENCRYPT_METHOD SHA512' /etc/login.defs; then
    echo "ENCRYPT_METHOD SHA512" | sudo tee -a /etc/login.defs
fi

if ! grep -q '^SHA_CRYPT_MIN_ROUNDS 5000' /etc/login.defs; then
    echo "SHA_CRYPT_MIN_ROUNDS 5000" | sudo tee -a /etc/login.defs
fi

if ! grep -q '^SHA_CRYPT_MAX_ROUNDS 5000' /etc/login.defs; then
    echo "SHA_CRYPT_MAX_ROUNDS 5000" | sudo tee -a /etc/login.defs
fi

# Update existing user passwords to use the new hashing settings
for user in $(cut -d: -f1 /etc/passwd); do
    current_hash=$(grep "^$user:" /etc/shadow | cut -d: -f2)
    if [[ $current_hash == *"\$6\$" ]]; then
        echo "$user" | sudo passwd --stdin "$user"
    fi
done

# Define the minimum password age in days
MIN_PASSWORD_AGE=1

# Get a list of all local user accounts
users=$(getent passwd | cut -d: -f1)

# Iterate over each user
for user in $users; do
    # Get the current password age for the user
    current_age=$(chage -l "$user" | grep "Password expires" | awk '{print $4}')
    
    # Check if the current age is less than the minimum required age
    if [[ $current_age -lt $MIN_PASSWORD_AGE ]]; then
        # Set the minimum password age for the user
        chage -m $MIN_PASSWORD_AGE "$user"
    fi
done

```bash
#!/bin/bash
```

# Define the desired permission mode
desired_mode="0740"

# Function to apply permissions to a file
apply_permissions() {
    local file="$1"
    local current_mode
    current_mode=$(stat -c "%a" "$file")
    if [[ "$current_mode" -gt "$desired_mode" ]]; then
        chmod "$desired_mode" "$file"
    fi
}

# Process root's initialization files
for file in /root/.bashrc /root/.bash_profile /root/.profile /root/.bash_login; do
    if [[ -f "$file" ]]; then
        apply_permissions "$file"
    fi
done

# Process other users' initialization files
while IFS=: read -r username _ uid _ _ homedir _; do
    if [[ "$uid" -ge 1000 && -d "$homedir" ]]; then
        for file in "$homedir"/.bashrc "$homedir"/.bash_profile "$homedir"/.profile "$homedir"/.bash_login; do
            if [[ -f "$file" ]]; then
                apply_permissions "$file"
            fi
        done
    fi
done < /etc/passwd

# Define the desired umask value
desired_umask="077"

# Check if the .cshrc file exists in the user's home directory
if [ -f ~/.cshrc ]; then
    # Check if the umask setting already exists in the .cshrc file
    if grep -q "^umask $desired_umask$" ~/.cshrc; then
        echo "Umask is already set to $desired_umask in ~/.cshrc."
    else
        # Add the umask setting to the .cshrc file
        echo "Setting umask to $desired_umask in ~/.cshrc."
        echo "umask $desired_umask" >> ~/.cshrc

else
    # Create the .cshrc file and set the umask
    echo "Creating ~/.cshrc and setting umask to $desired_umask."
    echo "umask $desired_umask" > ~/.cshrc
fi

# Apply the umask setting immediately for the current session
echo "Applying umask $desired_umask for the current session."
umask $desired_umask

```bash
# Define the desired umask value
desired_umask="077"
```

# Get the current umask value from /etc/login.defs

    echo "umask $desired_umask" > "$profile_d_script"

```bash
#!/bin/bash
```

# Ensure the script is run as root
if [[ $EUID -ne 0 ]]; then
    echo "This script must be run as root" >&2
    exit 1
fi

# Function to update GRUB configuration for a specific kernel
update_grub_for_kernel() {
    local kernel_version="$1"
    local grub_config="/etc/default/grub.d/99-kpti.cfg"

    # Check if the kernel version is already configured
    if grep -q "GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX_DEFAULT.*kpti=on" "$grub_config"; then
        echo "Kernel $kernel_version already has KPTI enabled."
        return
    fi

    # Add KPTI to the GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX_DEFAULT for the specified kernel
    echo "GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX_DEFAULT=\"\$GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX_DEFAULT kpti=on\"" > "$grub_config"
    echo "Updated GRUB configuration for kernel $kernel_version."

    # Update GRUB to apply changes
    grub2-mkconfig -o /boot/grub2/grub.cfg
}

# Get all installed kernel versions
kernel_versions=$(rpm -qa --queryformat '%{VERSION}-%{RELEASE}.%{ARCH}\n' kernel)

# Update GRUB configuration for each kernel version
for kernel_version in $kernel_versions; do
    update_grub_for_kernel "$kernel_version"
done

echo "KPTI has been enabled for all installed kernels and will persist across reboots."

generated_script:
#!/bin/bash

# Ensure the script is run as root
if [[ $EUID -ne 0 ]]; then
    echo "This script must be run as root" >&2
    exit 1
fi

# Function to update the GRUB configuration for a specific kernel
update_grub_for_kernel() {
    local kernel_version="$1"
    local grub_config="/etc/default/grub"
    local grub_entry="GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX_DEFAULT"

    # Check if the kernel version is valid
    if ! rpm -q kernel-"$kernel_version" &> /dev/null; then
        echo "Kernel version $kernel_version not found"
        return
    fi

    # Backup the current GRUB configuration
    cp "$grub_config" "$grub_config.bak"

    # Append 'nosysrq' to the GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX_DEFAULT if not already present
    if ! grep -q "nosysrq" "$grub_config"; then
        sed -i "s/^${grub_entry}=\"\([^\"]*\)\"/${grub_entry}=\"\1 nosysrq\"/" "$grub_config"
    fi

    # Update GRUB for the specific kernel version
    grub2-mkconfig -o "/boot/grub2/grub.cfg"
}

# Get all installed kernel versions
kernel_versions=$(rpm -qa --queryformat '%{VERSION}-%{RELEASE}.%{ARCH}\n' kernel | sort -V)

# Update GRUB configuration for each kernel version
for kernel_version in $kernel_versions; do
    update_grub_for_kernel "$kernel_version"
done

# Ensure the change is applied on next reboot
grub2-set-default 0

echo "GRUB configuration updated for all installed kernels. System will apply changes on next reboot."

```bash
    exit 1

update_grub_for_kernel() {
    local kernel_version="$1"
    local grub_config="/etc/default/grub"
    local grub_entry="GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX_DEFAULT"

    # Check if the kernel version is valid
    if ! rpm -q kernel-"$kernel_version" &> /dev/null; then
        echo "Kernel version $kernel_version not found"
        return
    fi

    # Backup the current GRUB configuration
    cp "$grub_config" "$grub_config.bak"

    # Append 'nosysrq' to the GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX_DEFAULT if not already present
    if ! grep -q "nosysrq" "$grub_config"; then
        sed -i "s/^${grub_entry}=\"\([^\"]*\)\"/${grub_entry}=\"\1 nosysrq\"/" "$grub_config"
    fi

    # Update GRUB for the specific kernel version
    grub2-mkconfig -o "/boot/grub2/grub.cfg"
}

# Get all installed kernel versions
kernel_versions=$(rpm -qa --queryformat '%{VERSION}-%{RELEASE}.%{ARCH}\n' kernel | sort -V)

# Update GRUB configuration for each kernel version
for kernel_version in $kernel_versions; do
    update_grub_for_kernel "$kernel_version"
done

# Ensure the change is applied on next reboot
grub2-set-default 0

echo "GRUB configuration updated for all installed kernels. System will apply changes on next reboot."
```

net.ipv6.conf.default.accept_ra = 0

#!/bin/bash

# Ensure sysctl configuration for disabling ICMP redirects
cat <<EOF | sudo tee /etc/sysctl.d/99-disable-icmp-redirect.conf > /dev/null
net.ipv4.conf.all.send_redirects = 0
net.ipv4.conf.default.send_redirects = 0
EOF

# Apply the sysctl settings immediately
sudo sysctl -p /etc/sysctl.d/99-disable-icmp-redirect.conf

# Verify the settings are applied
for interface in \$(ip -o link show | awk -F': ' '{print \$2}'); do
    current_value=\$(sysctl -n net.ipv4.conf.\${interface}.send_redirects)
    if [ "\${current_value}" -ne 0 ]; then
        echo "Failed to disable ICMP redirects on interface \${interface}"
        exit 1
    fi

```bash
#!/bin/bash

# Ensure sysctl configuration for disabling ICMP redirects
cat <<EOF | sudo tee /etc/sysctl.d/99-disable-icmp-redirect.conf > /dev/null
net.ipv4.conf.all.send_redirects = 0
net.ipv4.conf.default.send_redirects = 0
EOF

# Apply the sysctl settings immediately
sudo sysctl -p /etc/sysctl.d/99-disable-icmp-redirect.conf

# Verify the settings are applied
for interface in \$(ip -o link show | awk -F': ' '{print \$2}'); do
    current_value=\$(sysctl -n net.ipv4.conf.\${interface}.send_redirects)
    if [ "\${current_value}" -ne 0 ]; then
        echo "Failed to disable ICMP redirects on interface \${interface}"
        exit 1
    fi
done

echo "ICMP redirects disabled successfully on all IPv4 interfaces."
```

#!/bin/bash

# Ensure the CAN kernel module is blacklisted
if ! grep -q '^blacklist can' /etc/modprobe.d/blacklist.conf; then
    echo 'blacklist can' | sudo tee -a /etc/modprobe.d/blacklist.conf > /dev/null
fi

# Remove the CAN module if it's currently loaded
if lsmod | grep -q '^can\s'; then
    sudo rmmod can
fi

# Ensure the CAN device nodes are removed
for device in /dev/can*; do
    if [ -e "$device" ]; then
        sudo rm -f "$device"
    fi
done

# Ensure the CAN service is disabled and stopped if it exists
if systemctl list-unit-files | grep -q '^can.service'; then
    sudo systemctl disable can.service
    sudo systemctl stop can.service
fi

# Ensure the CAN service is not enabled to start on boot
sudo systemctl mask can.service

# Ensure the CAN service is not running
sudo systemctl stop can.service

# Ensure the CAN service is not installed
if rpm -qa | grep -q '^can'; then
    sudo yum remove -y can
fi

if ! grep -q "install sctp /bin/true" /etc/modprobe.d/blacklist.conf; then
    echo "install sctp /bin/true" | sudo tee -a /etc/modprobe.d/blacklist.conf > /dev/null
fi

# Remove any existing SCTP modules
for mod in $(lsmod | grep sctp | awk '{print $1}'); do
    sudo rmmod $mod
done

# Disable SCTP in sysctl if it's enabled
if sysctl -q net.sctp.enabled; then
    sudo sysctl -w net.sctp.enabled=0
    echo "net.sctp.enabled = 0" | sudo tee -a /etc/sysctl.d/99-disable-sctp.conf > /dev/null
fi

# Reload sysctl configuration
sudo sysctl --system

#!/bin/bash

# Function to check if a directory is on a separate filesystem
is_separate_filesystem() {
    local dir="$1"
    df "$dir" | awk 'NR==2 {print $1}' | grep -qE '^/dev/'
}

# Check if /boot is on a separate filesystem
if is_separate_filesystem /boot; then
    # Get the current mount options for /boot
    current_options=$(mount | grep ' on /boot ' | awk '{print $3}')
    
    # Check if noexec option is already set
    if [[ ! "$current_options" =~ noexec ]]; then
        # Add noexec option to /etc/fstab
        sed -i '/^\/dev\//s/\(.*\) \/boot .*/\1 \/boot \1,noexec/' /etc/fstab
        
        # Remount /boot with the new options
        mount -o remount,noexec /boot
    fi
else
    echo "Warning: /boot is not on a separate filesystem. No action taken."
fi

```bash
}
```

    # Check if the filesystem has the 'nodev' option

```bash
#!/bin/bash
```

# Define the group for privileged users
PRIVILEGED_GROUP="wheel"

# Check if the group exists, if not, create it
getent group "$PRIVILEGED_GROUP" >/dev/null || groupadd "$PRIVILEGED_GROUP"

# Change ownership of /dev/kmsg to root:wheel
chown root:"$PRIVILEGED_GROUP" /dev/kmsg

# Set permissions on /dev/kmsg to allow read access only for the privileged group
chmod g+r,o-r /dev/kmsg

# Ensure the changes persist across reboots by adding the following lines to /etc/security/limits.conf
if ! grep -q "^@wheel.*kmem.*$" /etc/security/limits.conf; then
    echo "@wheel soft kmem -1" >> /etc/security/limits.conf
    echo "@wheel hard kmem -1" >> /etc/security/limits.conf
fi

# Apply the changes immediately
setcap cap_syslog=ep /sbin/syslogd

```bash
#!/bin/bash
```

# Ensure the script is run as root
if [[ $EUID -ne 0 ]]; then
    echo "This script must be run as root"
    exit 1
fi

# Define the kernel parameter to add
KERNEL_PARAM="page_poison=1"

# Function to update GRUB configuration
update_grub() {
    local GRUB_CFG="/etc/default/grub"
    local GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=$(grep -oP '(?<=GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=").*?(?=")' "$GRUB_CFG")

    # Check if the parameter is already set
    if [[ $GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX != *"$KERNEL_PARAM"* ]]; then
        # Append the parameter to the existing GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX
        GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX="$GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX $KERNEL_PARAM"
        sed -i "s|GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=\".*\"|GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=\"$GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX\"|" "$GRUB_CFG"
        grub2-mkconfig -o /boot/grub2/grub.cfg
    fi
}

# Update GRUB for all currently installed kernels
for kernel in $(rpm -q kernel); do
    update_grub
done

# Ensure the parameter is added to future kernel installations
cat <<EOF | tee /etc/dracut.conf.d/page_poison.conf
kernel_cmdline+=" $KERNEL_PARAM"
EOF

# Regenerate initramfs for all currently installed kernels
for kernel in $(rpm -q kernel); do
    dracut --force --kver "$kernel"
done

echo "Memory management hardening with free-page poisoning has been enabled."

```bash
#!/bin/bash

# Install necessary packages
yum install -y selinux-policy-devel policycoreutils-python-utils

# Create a new SELinux module for application allowlisting
cat <<EOF > /tmp/app_allowlist.te
module app_allowlist 1.0;

require {
    type unconfined_t;
    class file { execute execute_no_trans };
}

# Deny all file executions by default
allow unconfined_t unconfined_t:file execute;
allow unconfined_t unconfined_t:file execute_no_trans;

# Permit specific applications (add your trusted applications here)
allow unconfined_t my_trusted_app_t:file execute;
allow unconfined_t my_trusted_app_t:file execute_no_trans;
EOF

# Compile the SELinux module
checkmodule -M -m -o /tmp/app_allowlist.mod /tmp/app_allowlist.te
semodule_package -o /tmp/app_allowlist.pp -m /tmp/app_allowlist.mod

# Install the SELinux module
semodule -i /tmp/app_allowlist.pp

# Remove temporary files
rm -f /tmp/app_allowlist.te /tmp/app_allowlist.mod /tmp/app_allowlist.pp

# Set SELinux to enforcing mode
setenforce 1

# Ensure SELinux is set to enforcing on reboot
sed -i 's/^SELINUX=.*$/SELINUX=enforcing/' /etc/selinux/config

# Reload SELinux policies
restorecon -R /

# Print completion message
echo "Application allowlisting configuration completed successfully."
```

if [ "$CURRENT_INTERVAL" -ne "$CLIENT_ALIVE_INTERVAL" ] || [ "$CURRENT_COUNT_MAX" -ne "$CLIENT_ALIVE_COUNT_MAX" ]; then
    sed -i "/^ClientAliveInterval/c\ClientAliveInterval $CLIENT_ALIVE_INTERVAL" "$SSH_CONFIG"
    sed -i "/^ClientAliveCountMax/c\ClientAliveCountMax $CLIENT_ALIVE_COUNT_MAX" "$SSH_CONFIG"
    systemctl restart sshd
fi

    echo "$option $value $comment" >> "$SSH_CONFIG"

systemctl restart sshd

systemctl enable sshd

```bash
#!/bin/bash
```

# Ensure the SSSD configuration file exists
SSSD_CONF="/etc/sssd/sssd.conf"
if [ ! -f "$SSSD_CONF" ]; then
    echo "SSSD configuration file not found at $SSSD_CONF"
    exit 1
fi

# Backup the original configuration file
cp -n "$SSSD_CONF" "$SSSD_CONF.bak"

# Function to update SSSD configuration
update_sssd_conf() {
    local key="$1"
    local value="$2"
    local section="$3"

    # Check if the section exists, if not, add it
    if ! grep -q "\[$section\]" "$SSSD_CONF"; then
        echo "[$section]" >> "$SSSD_CONF"
    fi

    # Update or add the key-value pair in the specified section
    sed -i "/^\[$section\]$/,/^\[/ s/^$key\s*=.*/$key = $value/" "$SSSD_CONF"
    if ! grep -q "^$key\s*=" "$SSSD_CONF"; then
        echo "$key = $value" >> "$SSSD_CONF"
    fi
}

# Enable OCSP-based certificate status checking
update_sssd_conf "pam_cert_auth" "True" "pam"
update_sssd_conf "ocsp_enabled" "True" "pam"

# Restart SSSD to apply changes
systemctl restart sssd

# Verify that SSSD is running
if ! systemctl is-active --quiet sssd; then
    echo "Failed to restart SSSD"
    exit 1
fi

echo "OCSP-based certificate status checking has been enabled in SSSD."

yum install -y pam_pkcs11 nss-tools

# Configure PAM for smart card authentication
if ! grep -q "pam_pkcs11.so" /etc/pam.d/system-auth; then
    sed -i '/auth\s\+required\s\+pam_unix.so/a auth        sufficient    pam_pkcs11.so' /etc/pam.d/system-auth
fi

if ! grep -q "pam_pkcs11.so" /etc/pam.d/password-auth; then
    sed -i '/auth\s\+required\s\+pam_unix.so/a auth        sufficient    pam_pkcs11.so' /etc/pam.d/password-auth
fi

# Enable and start the pcscd service
systemctl enable pcscd
systemctl start pcscd

# Configure NSS to trust the smart card certificates
if ! grep -q "smartcard" /etc/pki/nssdb/pkcs11.txt; then
    echo "library=libnssckbi.so" >> /etc/pki/nssdb/pkcs11.txt
    echo "name=Smart Card" >> /etc/pki/nssdb/pkcs11.txt
    echo "parameters=sql:/usr/lib64/pkcs11/smartcard.db" >> /etc/pki/nssdb/pkcs11.txt
fi

# Restart the system to apply changes
reboot

# Ensure USBGuard is installed
if ! rpm -q usbguard &> /dev/null; then
    echo "USBGuard is not installed. Please install it first."
    exit 1
fi

# Generate a new policy file
POLICY_FILE="/etc/usbguard/rules.conf"
TEMP_POLICY_FILE=$(mktemp)

# Allow all currently connected devices
usbguard list-devices --output-script > "$TEMP_POLICY_FILE"

# Replace the existing policy file with the new one
mv "$TEMP_POLICY_FILE" "$POLICY_FILE"

# Reload USBGuard to apply the new policy
usbguard reload

# Ensure the policy file is owned by root and has the correct permissions
chown root:root "$POLICY_FILE"
chmod 644 "$POLICY_FILE"

# Enable and start USBGuard service to persist across reboots
systemctl enable usbguard

```bash
fi
```

#!/bin/bash

# Define the desired audit backlog limit
desired_backlog_limit=8192

# Function to update the audit backlog limit in the GRUB configuration
update_grub_audit_backlog() {
    local grub_config="/etc/default/grub"
    local updated_grub_config="/tmp/grub.cfg.updated"

    # Check if the GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX line exists and contains audit_backlog_limit
    if grep -q "GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=" "$grub_config" && grep -q "audit_backlog_limit=" "$grub_config"; then
        # Update the existing audit_backlog_limit value
        sed "s/audit_backlog_limit=[0-9]\+/audit_backlog_limit=$desired_backlog_limit/" "$grub_config" > "$updated_grub_config"
    else
        # Append audit_backlog_limit to the existing GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX line
        sed "/GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX=/ s/$/ audit_backlog_limit=$desired_backlog_limit/" "$grub_config" > "$updated_grub_config"
    fi

    # Replace the original GRUB configuration file with the updated one
    mv "$updated_grub_config" "$grub_config"
}

# Apply the update to the current GRUB configuration
update_grub_audit_backlog

# Update GRUB to apply the changes
grub2-mkconfig -o /boot/grub2/grub.cfg

# Ensure the change persists across reboots by updating the GRUB configuration for all kernels
for kernel_version in $(rpm -qa --queryformat '%{VERSION}-%{RELEASE}.%{ARCH}\n' kernel); do
    grub2-mkconfig -o /boot/grub2/grub.cfg
done

    echo "$audit_rule" | sudo tee /etc/audit/rules.d/audit.rules.d/custom_lsetxattr.rules > /dev/null

}

    add_audit_rule_augenrules
else
    add_audit_rule_auditctl
fi

# Ensure the rules persist across reboots
sudo systemctl restart auditd

AUDIT_RULE_32='-a always,exit -F arch=b32 -S open,openat,open_by_handle_at -F exit=-EACCES -F exit=-EPERM -F auid>=1000 -k access'

```bash
if ! rpm -q audit; then
```

disk_error_action = SYSLOG
EOF

#!/bin/bash

systemctl enable auditd

#!/bin/bash

# Define the audit log directory
AUDIT_LOG_DIR="/var/log/audit"

# Define the threshold for low disk space (in percentage)
LOW_SPACE_THRESHOLD=25

# Function to check disk space and trigger notification if below threshold
check_disk_space() {
    local available_space=$(df -h "$AUDIT_LOG_DIR" | awk 'NR==2 {print $5}' | sed 's/%//')
    if (( available_space <= LOW_SPACE_THRESHOLD )); then
        echo "Audit log directory is running low on disk space: $available_space% available"
        # Trigger the low-space notification action here
        # For example, you can send an email or log a message
        # Example: logger -p local0.warning "Audit log directory is running low on disk space: $available_space% available"
    fi
}

# Ensure the audit daemon is configured to use the specified log directory
if ! grep -q "^log_file = $AUDIT_LOG_DIR/audit.log$" /etc/audit/auditd.conf; then
    echo "log_file = $AUDIT_LOG_DIR/audit.log" >> /etc/audit/auditd.conf
fi

# Create a systemd service to run the disk space check periodically
cat <<EOF | sudo tee /etc/systemd/system/audit-disk-check.service > /dev/null
[Unit]
Description=Audit Log Disk Space Check
After=auditd.service

[Service]
Type=oneshot
ExecStart=/bin/bash -c '$(declare -f check_disk_space); check_disk_space'
RemainAfterExit=true

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target
EOF

# Create a systemd timer to run the disk space check every hour
cat <<EOF | sudo tee /etc/systemd/system/audit-disk-check.timer > /dev/null
[Unit]
Description=Run Audit Log Disk Space Check Hourly

[Timer]
OnCalendar=*-*-* *:00:00
Persistent=true

[Install]
WantedBy=timers.target
EOF

# Enable and start the timer
sudo systemctl daemon-reload
sudo systemctl enable audit-disk-check.timer
sudo systemctl start audit-disk-check.timer

```bash
# Function to check disk space and trigger notification if below threshold
check_disk_space() {
    local available_space=$(df -h "$AUDIT_LOG_DIR" | awk 'NR==2 {print $5}' | sed 's/%//')
    if (( available_space <= LOW_SPACE_THRESHOLD )); then
        echo "Audit log directory is running low on disk space: $available_space% available"
        # Trigger the low-space notification action here
        # For example, you can send an email or log a message
        # Example: logger -p local0.warning "Audit log directory is running low on disk space: $available_space% available"
    fi
}

# Ensure the audit daemon is configured to use the specified log directory
if ! grep -q "^log_file = $AUDIT_LOG_DIR/audit.log$" /etc/audit/auditd.conf; then
    echo "log_file = $AUDIT_LOG_DIR/audit.log" >> /etc/audit/auditd.conf
fi

# Create a systemd service to run the disk space check periodically
cat <<EOF | sudo tee /etc/systemd/system/audit-disk-check.service > /dev/null
[Unit]
Description=Audit Log Disk Space Check
After=auditd.service

[Service]
Type=oneshot
ExecStart=/bin/bash -c '$(declare -f check_disk_space); check_disk_space'
RemainAfterExit=true

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target
EOF

# Create a systemd timer to run the disk space check every hour
cat <<EOF | sudo tee /etc/systemd/system/audit-disk-check.timer > /dev/null
[Unit]
Description=Run Audit Log Disk Space Check Hourly

[Timer]
OnCalendar=*-*-* *:00:00
Persistent=true

[Install]
WantedBy=timers.target
EOF

# Enable and start the timer
sudo systemctl daemon-reload
sudo systemctl enable audit-disk-check.timer
sudo systemctl start audit-disk-check.timer
```

#!/bin/bash

if ! rpm -q audit; then
    yum install -y audit
fi

# Configure auditd to use the fully qualified domain name (FQDN) as the node name
auditctl -e 0  # Disable auditd to make changes
sed -i '/^name_format/s/^.*$/name_format = hostname/' /etc/audit/auditd.conf
auditctl -e 1  # Enable auditd after making changes

# Ensure the configuration persists across reboots
systemctl enable auditd

fi

```bash
#!/bin/bash

# Define the directory containing audit rule files
AUDIT_RULE_DIR="/etc/audit/rules.d"

# Find all files in the audit rule directory and set their permissions to 0600
find "$AUDIT_RULE_DIR" -type f -name "*.rules" -exec chmod 0600 {} \;
```
